prompt: ingredients:bread, chicken, cheese, eggs@cuisine:chinese@maxCalories:500@maxCookTime:30@servings:2@pantry:available@difficulty:easy
generated_text:
Malaysian-Style Rice and Sweet Potato Bowl

Ingredients:

- 2 cups of long-grain white rice
- 2 potatoes, chopped into small cubes
- 2 tablespoons of raz-el-hanout spice blend
- 1 aubergine, diced
- 1 courgette, diced
- 1 sweet potato, diced
- 1/2 teaspoon of salt
- 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (205 degrees C).

2. Rinse the rice under cold water until it is no longer cloudy and then let it sit in cold water for 10 minutes.

3. Drain the rice and add to a pot with 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then lower heat to medium-low and let simmer for 20 minutes.

4. In a separate bowl, combine potatoes, raz-el-hanout, aubergine and courgette. Toss with 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.

5. Place the vegetables on a lightly greased baking sheet and bake in preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown. 

6. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add in the sweet potato and season with salt. Sauté for 5 minutes.

7. When the rice is done, add it to the pot with the sweet potato and stir to combine. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.

8. Serve the rice and sweet potato immediately with the roasted vegetables on top. Enjoy!

[instructions]:In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic and stir.@Add rice and broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and season with salt. [ingredients]:4 oz mince beef@1/2 head of cauliflower (florets only)@2 carrots (diced)@1/3 cup cherry tomatoes (halved)@2 cups cooked pasta of your choice@2 tsp olive oil@1 pinch salt@1 pinch black pepper
[instructions]: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. @Add the mince beef and cook until browned. @Stir in the carrots and cauliflower, cooking them for 5 minutes or until just starting to soften. @Add the cherry tomatoes and cook for an additional two minutes or until they are softened. @Stir in the cooked pasta and season with salt and pepper. @Mix everything together and let simmer for 3-5 minutes. Serve hot! Bon appetit! 

[calories]:420 cals/serving
[servings]:2 
[cook_time]:15 minutes

[recipe]:Portuguese Fruit and Vegetable Salad
[ingredients]:1 banana (sliced)@1 kiwi (skinned, halved and sliced)@1 apple (cored, peeled and diced)@3 cups of lettuce leaves (torn into pieces)@1/2 small aubergine (diced)@1 pear (sliced)@1/4 cup reduced-fat milk@1 tablespoon olive oil@1 teaspoon dried oregano@salt & pepper to taste 

[instructions]:In a large bowl mix together all the fruit and vegetables.@ In a separate bowl whisk together the milk, olive oil, oregano, salt, and pepper.@Pour the dressing over the salad and toss well to combine. @Refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow flavours to infuse. Served chilled. Enjoy!(random)

[recipe]:Portuguese Apple and Banana Salad
[ingredients]:1 banana, cut into small cubes@1 kiwi fruit, sliced@1 apple, cubed@2 handfuls of salad greens (lettuce, spinach or rocket)@2 aubergines, diced@1 pear, sliced thinly@150ml cold skimmed milk@1 tsp olive oil@1 pinch ground cloves@1 pinch black pepper
[instructions]:Add the banana, kiwi, apple and salad greens to a bowl and mix. @Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat and add the aubergine and cook until brown.@Add the pear pieces and an extra pinch of ground clove, then pour in the raced milk. Simmer for 5 minutes.@Ladle the hot mixture over the salad ingredients and season with ground pepper. Toss lightly and serve.
[calories]:250 cals/serving
[servings]:2
[cook_time]:15 minutes

[recipe]:Malaysian-Style Biryani
[ingredients]:2 cups Basmati rice@1 potato (diced)@1 aubergine (cubed)@ 1 courgette (sliced)@½ sweet potato (diced)@3 tablespoons raz el hanout@ 2 tablespoons olive oil@2 pinches of salt@2 pinches of pepper
[instructions]:Cook the Basmati rice according to the instructions on the package. Set aside when done.@In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and add the diced potatoes and sweet potatoes. Fry for a few minutes until golden brown. @Add the aubergines and courgettes and sauté for another few minutes until softened.@Stir in the Raz el Hanout spice mix and season with salt and pepper.@Gently fold in the cooked Basmati rice. Reduce the heat to low and gently stir everything together.@Cover the pan and let simmer for 10–15 minutes until the vegetables are tender.@{Serve hot and enjoy!}
[calories]:484 cals/serving
[servings]:2
[cook_time]:25 minutes

[recipe]:Malaysian Fried Rice
[ingredients]:1 tbsp vegetable oil@3 cups cooked rice@3/4 cup potato (cut into small cubes)@3/4 cup aubergine (chopped)@3/4 cup courgette (chopped)@1/2 cup sweet potato (cubed)@2 tsp raz-el-hanout@1 pinch salt@1 pinch black pepper
[instructions]:Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat.@Add the potatoes and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. @Add the sweet potato, aubergine, and courgette to the pan and stir fry until tender.@Stir in the cooked rice and raz-el-hanout spice mix.@Season with salt and pepper.@Fry everything together for a few minutes before serving hot. Enjoy!
[calories]:406 cals/serving
[servings]:2
[cook_time]:15 minutes
